privacy.title: Privacy and AI terms of use
privacy.storage: To protect your privacy, questions containing personal information (like phone numbers or email addresses) are blocked. You'll see sensitive content marked with XXX and be asked to rephrase. These and other blocked questions (for example, questions that include profanity or are in an unrecognized language or code) are not sent to our AI service or stored. All other questions, answers and your feedback are stored to improve system performance.
privacy.disclaimer:
Use the Government of Canada website link provided in the response to check your answer. Responses generated by this AI system should not be considered as professional, legal, or medical advice.

We attempt to ensure the accuracy of the information provided but there is a possibility that the information may contain inaccuracies, and the information may not yet reflect recent changes or fulfill your particular needs or purposes.
privacy.language: The Official Languages Act, the Official Languages (Communications with and Services to the Public) Regulations and Treasury Board policy requirements establish when we use both English and French to provide services to members of the public. Choose the English or French language button at the top of the page to use the corresponding official language version of AI Answers. Questions asked and answered in a language other than English or French are provided for the convenience of our visitors.
privacy.terms:
This AI system relies on information provided on Government of Canada websites. The Government of Canada does not provide legal advice.

Your use of this system and any information generated is also subject to the

processed.title: Completed batches
processed.description: Missing questions? Only questions that pass the privacy/threat/manipulation guardrails are answered, stored in the database and exported into the output file.

instructions.tipsTitle: Tips for processing and evaluating results
instructions.step1Summary: How to create a CSV file
instructions.step1a: At minimum, a column with your questions labelled QUESTION and one with your referring URL labelled URL.
instructions.step1b: The questions can be numbered with a space before the question text, for example, 001. How to apply for EI.
instructions.step1c: Your CSV file can contain other columns, such as previous answers. Use the question numbers to match them up later.
instructions.step1d: You'll need separate files for English questions to be asked/answered as if on an English page, and French questions to be asked/answered on the French AI Answers page.
instructions.step1e: A batch can contain a maximum of 200 questions. Split larger sets into multiple files.
instructions.step3a: Select the language of the batch file.
instructions.step3b: (i.e. the language of the page the batch is simulating)
instructions.reviewIncomplete: After a successful upload, initiate processing and/or check on your batch's progress below.
instructions.incompleteLinkText: Go to the Incomplete batches section
instructions.step6: When it's done (if it gets interrupted and didn't finish, click the Process button again), it will move to the Completed batches section. You can download the file from there.
instructions.step7: All of the chats run in the batch will be available to evaluate in the Evaluation dashboard and Chat viewer with the email address of the person who submitted the batch.

database.title: Database management
database.createIndexesDescription: Ensure that all defined indexes exist in the database. This is useful if indexes were dropped or new schemas were deployed.
database.createIndexesConfirm: This will ensure all Mongoose indexes exist in the database. Are you sure you want to continue?
database.dropIndexesConfirm: This will drop all database indexes. Database operations may be slower until indexes are rebuilt automatically. Are you sure you want to continue?
database.deleteSystemLogsConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete all logs with chatId = "system"? This cannot be undone.
database.deleteAllBatchesConfirm: This will delete ALL batches and batchItems. This cannot be undone. Are you sure you want to continue?
database.repairTimestampsConfirm: This will add updatedAt timestamps to existing tool records without them. Are you sure you want to continue?
database.repairExpertFeedbackConfirm: This will set the "type" field to "expert" for expert feedback records that have missing or empty type fields. Records with "public" or "ai" types will be left unchanged. Are you sure you want to continue?
database.repairExpertFeedbackSuccess: Expert feedback types repaired successfully. Updated: {updated}/{total} ({alreadyCorrect} already correct)
database.migratePublicFeedbackConfirm: This will migrate all public feedback from expert feedback to the new public feedback collection. Are you sure you want to continue?
database.dropIndexesDescription: Remove all database indexes. This can be useful to fix database performance issues. Indexes will be automatically rebuilt by the database as needed.
database.indexStatusDescription: Check the current status of all database indexes across all collections.
database.indexCreationFailed: Index creation failed for the following collections:
database.deleteSystemLogsDescription: Delete all logs where chatId = "system". This action cannot be undone.
database.repairTimestampsDescription: Add updatedAt timestamps to existing tool records without them. This will use the createdAt date if available, or the current date as fallback.
database.deleteAllBatchesDescription: Delete all batch records and their associated batchItems. This is destructive and cannot be undone.
database.repairExpertFeedbackDescription: Set the "type" field to "expert" for expert feedback records that have missing or empty type fields. Records with "public" or "ai" types will be left unchanged.
database.migratePublicFeedbackDescription: Move all public feedback from the expert feedback collection to the new public feedback collection.
database.importDescription: Restore the database from a backup file. Warning: This will replace all existing data.
database.integrityDescription: Run read-only checks to find orphaned documents and parent records that reference missing children.
database.removeDuplicatesConfirm: This will delete older duplicate records, keeping only the newest. Are you sure?

checks.orphanCitations: Orphaned citations (not referenced by any answer)
checks.orphanTools: Orphaned tools (not referenced by any answer)
checks.orphanAnswers: Orphaned answers (not referenced by any interaction or embedding)
checks.orphanQuestions: Orphaned questions (not referenced by any interaction or embedding)
checks.orphanInteractions: Orphaned interactions (not referenced by any chat)
checks.sentenceEmbeddingOrphans: Sentence embeddings with missing parent embedding

evalPage.similarityDescription: This approach automatically evaluates new interactions by finding similar expert-evaluated interactions and transferring feedback scores and explanations. If sentence-level matching fails, the system will fall back to using a highly similar question+answer match with a high expert score.

initialValidation.title: Initial Validation
initialValidation.description: Validate the interaction has question and answer content and check whether an evaluation already exists.

embeddingRetrieval.title: Embedding Retrieval
embeddingRetrieval.description: Retrieve vector embeddings for question+answer, answer-only, and sentence-level embeddings.

findingSimilar.title: Finding Similar Content
findingSimilar.description: Search for interactions with existing expert feedback and strong QA similarity; filter out same-chat results and ensure sentence embeddings exist for candidates.

item.maxMatches: Returns up to 20 closest matches (candidates are re-sorted by similarity)

item.findMostSimilar: For each sentence in the new interaction, find the most similar sentence in each potential match (vector search with per-sentence threshold)
item.threshold: Keep matches above the sentence similarity threshold and prefer highest-similarity neighbors
item.transfer: If all or a sufficient number of sentences are matched, transfer sentence-level feedback and create a detailed evaluation
item.telemetry: Optionally run a sentence-compare agent for extra verification and capture telemetry (provider/model/latency/tokens)

item.compare: Compare the source citation URL with candidate interactions to find an exact or high-confidence citation match
item.score: Score and record a citation match (score, explanation, matched interaction/chat ids)
item.searchPage: Search page citations may be handled specially and scored zero

item.checkTop: If sentence-level matching fails, check the top QA matches (configurable top-N) for those with high expert feedback scores
item.citationCheck: For candidates, perform a citation match and optionally run a fallback compare agent to ensure the candidate answer sufficiently covers the source
item.useQaOnly: If a candidate passes checks, create an evaluation using the QA match (QA-high-score fallback) and record fallback metadata and candidate traces

item.agent: A small compare agent can be invoked to verify that a fallback candidate sufficiently matches the source answer; results, raw output and parsed checks are recorded
item.record: Whether compare was used and its meta (provider/model/latency/tokens) are stored on the evaluation for traceability

item.createFeedback: Create a new expert feedback object based on the matched interaction's feedback or generated fallback feedback (type 'ai')
item.computeScore: Compute total score from per-sentence scores and citation score (default weights applied). If no ratings exist, totalScore may be null.
item.mapFeedback: Map feedback to the new interaction (sentence-level or QA-only) and save sentence match trace and similarity scores
item.recordSimilarities: Record similarity scores, matched citation interaction/chat ids, fallback metadata, and a detailed stage timeline for auditing

item.recordNoMatch: If neither sentence-level nor QA fallback matches are accepted, create a no-match evaluation recording reason types and per-sentence rejection causes
item.trace: No-match evaluations include a sentence-trace and timeline so operators can inspect why candidates were rejected

item.record: The worker records a stage-by-stage timeline (stage, status, code, message, timestamp) to the evaluation for diagnostics
item.telemetry: Agent and VectorService telemetry (latency, tokens, model) are captured where applicable

scenarioOverrides.title: Edit and test scenarios
scenarioOverrides.description1: Click Edit for your department's scenario, make your changes, and when you're ready to test, check the Apply edits box. Make sure you haven't added ambiguity! Keep it terse to reduce cognitive load on the AI. To test, click Back to admin, and select the top 'Use AI Answers...' option. It will apply your changes locally so that you can test. Test with multiple questions multiple times.
scenarioOverrides.description2: If you're happy with the changes, alert the AI Answers team, copy and paste the edited version for them, and they'll make and push the changes into the live AI Answers product.

datasets.title: Experimental Datasets
datasets.goldenAnswerDescription: Create a question and answer dataset from chats with a 100% expert evaluation in the selected date range.
datasets.instantAnswerDescription: Create an instant-answer test dataset from first-turn questions with a 100% expert evaluation. The original question is retained and the language model creates strictly equivalent wording variants with the same golden answer.
datasets.occurrencesPerQuestionHint: Includes the original question. Choose from 1 to 10.
datasets.instantAnswerRowCount: Eligible first-turn questions: {sources}. Rows to add: {rows}.
datasets.creatingInstantAnswer: Generating equivalent questions and creating dataset...
datasets.goldenAnswerFailed: Failed to create golden answer dataset.
datasets.instantAnswerSuccess: Instant answer test dataset created successfully.
datasets.instantAnswerFailed: Failed to create instant answer test dataset.
datasets.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this dataset? This action cannot be undone.
datasets.inUse: This dataset is in use by active batches and cannot be deleted.
datasets.categoryPlaceholder: e.g. cra, ircc, bias, pii, red-team
datasets.columnAliasHint: Column names are matched without regard to case, spaces, hyphens or underscores. Extra columns are kept and ignored unless they conflict with the selected dataset type.

uploadDescriptions.questionOnly: Use this when you want the system to generate a fresh answer during analysis. Do not include an answer or reference answer column.
uploadDescriptions.qaPair: Use this when the dataset already contains the reference answer for each question. For comparison analyzers, the answer column is used as the reference and the system generates a fresh answer to compare against it.

uploadColumns.qaPair: question and answer (required), reference answer (optional), referringUrl (optional), chatId (optional)

analysis.title: Batch analysis
analysis.baselineHint: Pick a previous analysis to act as the comparison reference. When selected, new runs are compared against that run's answers.
analysis.referenceHint: If no reference run is selected, the dataset reference is used automatically. For question and answer datasets, the answer column is used as the reference for comparison analyzers.
analysis.expertScorerDescription: Judges the current answer against the dataset's canonical reference when one is available. It checks key ideas, recommendations, answer type, Government of Canada source integrity, XML tagging, unsupported claims, and answer quality. With a previous run selected, the main verdict still uses the dataset reference and a separate drift result describes whether the current answer improved, regressed, or stayed unchanged.
analysis.expertScorerInfo: Expert scorer requires a reference answer from the dataset or a comparison run. Use a question and answer dataset with a referenceAnswer column, or a comparison run.
analysis.expertScorerBaselineHint: Expert scorer will use the dataset reference answer for the main quality score and compare the current answer with the selected reference run for drift.
analysis.baselineOverridesReference: Warning: this dataset has a reference answer column, but the selected reference run's answers will replace it as the comparison reference for this run. Results will measure drift from that run, not against the dataset reference answer. Choose "No reference run" to compare against the dataset reference answer.

always.flagged: Nothing is flagged; the run only validates generation and storage.

datasetOnly.comparison: Current answer against the dataset reference answer.
datasetOnly.flagged: Missing key ideas, changed recommendations, unsupported claims, answer-type regressions, invalid tagging, or materially weaker quality.

datasetAndRun.comparison: Main verdict against the dataset reference; separate drift result against the previous run.
datasetAndRun.flagged: The main verdict can be flagged for quality issues. Drift is reported separately as improved, regressed, unchanged, or needs review.

standalone.comparison: No answer comparison; the run can become a future baseline.

withReference.comparison: Current answer against the supplied reference answer.
withReference.flagged: Material changes to facts, dates, numbers, eligibility, steps, named programs, scope, or URLs. Harmless wording changes are ignored.

standalone.comparison: Current answer is checked for recognized refusal signals.
standalone.flagged: The answer fails to refuse when the refusal evaluator expects a refusal.

withReference.comparison: Current refusal behavior against the reference refusal behavior.
withReference.flagged: An expected refusal is missing or refusal behavior changes relative to the reference.

standalone.comparison: Current answer is evaluated for harmful, unsafe, privacy, or other restricted content.
standalone.flagged: The answer contains a safety concern requiring review.

withReference.comparison: Current safety status and, separately, change from the reference.
withReference.flagged: A current safety concern or a meaningful safety regression can be flagged.

standalone.comparison: Current answer is evaluated for harmful bias.
standalone.flagged: The answer is classified as biased or caution-worthy.

withReference.comparison: Current bias level and change from the reference bias level.
withReference.flagged: A current bias concern or a change in bias level; detail-only differences are ignored.

analysis.datasetHelper: Upload and manage datasets through the Datasets page, then select one here.
analysis.empty: No previous runs found for this dataset.

messages.selectData: Please select a dataset or upload a file.
messages.selectDataset: Please select an existing dataset before starting analysis.
messages.comparisonRequired: Comparison analysis requires a second file or a specific dataset type.
messages.startingRun: Starting your analysis run. Please wait a moment.
messages.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this batch?
messages.error.NO_REFERENCE: This analyzer requires a reference answer: use a dataset with a referenceAnswer column, or select a comparison run.
messages.error.NO_REFERENCE_EXPERT_SCORER: Expert scorer requires a reference answer to judge against: use a dataset with a referenceAnswer column, or select a comparison run.
messages.error.NO_REFERENCE_INSTANT_ANSWER: Instant-answer validation requires a golden reference answer: use a dataset with an answer or referenceAnswer column.
messages.error.WORKFLOW_NO_INSTANT_ANSWER: Instant-answer validation requires a workflow with an instant-answer step.
messages.error.NO_ITEMS: Batch has no rows to process.
messages.error.BAD_REQUEST: Invalid request. Please check your configuration.
messages.error.INVALID_STATE: Batch is in an invalid state for this action.
messages.error.DUPLICATE: A resource with this name already exists.
messages.error.IN_USE: This resource is in use and cannot be deleted.

no-analyzer.name: No scoring
no-analyzer.description: This option runs the batch without any scoring pass. It is useful when you want to validate the batch pipeline, exports, or storage path without comparing answers or generating flags. Because nothing is being evaluated, status stays pass and flagged remains false. There is no reference comparison, no drift detection, and no regression logic. Use this mode when you only want to confirm that the workflow can move data from input to output, not when you need quality review.

bias-detection.name: Bias
bias-detection.description: This analyzer checks whether the answer introduces or reinforces harmful bias, for example bias tied to gender, race, age, disability, or other protected characteristics. It looks for language that stereotypes, excludes, or treats a group unfairly, and it can also compare the current run with a reference run to see if the bias got better, worse, or stayed the same. A flagged result means the answer contains a bias signal that deserves review, or that the current answer is materially more biased than the reference. If no reference exists, the analyzer behaves like a standalone safety review and reports the result of the current answer only. Use this when you need to watch for harmful framing, unfair assumptions, or subtle regressions in tone and wording.

expert-scorer.name: Expert scorer
expert-scorer.description:
Judges answer quality against Government of Canada expectations. It can score a question and answer pair on its own, or compare that answer against a reference answer or reference run when you provide one. What each answer is judged against depends on how you set up the run:
• Your dataset has a reference answer column — each answer is judged against the expert-approved reference: missing key ideas, changed recommendations, and answer-type regressions (for example, a normal answer becoming a refusal). A fail means the answer is wrong or weaker than the expert standard.
• You selected a reference run — the same judgment, but the reference is that run's generated answers, so a fail means quality changed since that run, not that the answer is wrong. If the dataset also has a reference answer, the reference run's answers take precedence.
Use this as the default scorer when you want a broad quality judgment, whether or not you have a comparison answer.

refusal.name: Refusal
refusal.description: This analyzer checks whether an expected refusal is preserved, including prompt-tagged refusals such as <not-gc> and <pt-muni>, short-query blocks, safety blocks, and other error-driven refusal states. A flagged result means the reference refused but the current answer responded normally. In comparison mode, it checks whether refusal behaviour changed between the reference and the current run.

safety.name: Safety
safety.description: This analyzer reviews the answer for harmful content, hate speech, unsafe instructions, privacy leaks, and other content that should not be released as-is. A flagged result means the answer crossed a safety boundary or exposed content that should be examined before the run is considered acceptable. When a reference is provided, the analyzer compares the two runs and reports whether safety improved, regressed, or stayed stable. Use this analyzer to monitor moderation quality, catch risky outputs early, and confirm that changes to the system are making responses safer instead of less safe.

similar-answer.name: Similar answer
similar-answer.description:
A strict fact-by-fact difference detector: it compares two answers and flags changes in dates, numbers, eligibility rules, steps, named programs, scope, and URLs that would change what a user should do. Harmless rewording and formatting changes are ignored, and it returns needs-review when unsure. What it compares depends on how you set up the run:
• You selected a reference run — each new answer is compared against that run's answer to the same question. A flag means the answer changed since that run — it may have changed for the better. This is the usual drift-monitoring setup.
• Your dataset has a reference answer column — each generated answer is compared against the expert-approved reference, flagging factual deviations only. For a broader quality judgment against a reference answer, prefer the Expert scorer.
• Neither — there is nothing to compare: the run only captures answers and is not scored (the suite grid marks it as a reference capture). Select it as the reference for a later drift run.
Use this to catch subtle factual regressions that a general quality review might miss.

instant-answer.name: Instant-answer validation
instant-answer.description: Checks that an instant-answer workflow used its short-circuit step and returned an answer identical to the golden reference. The detailed result includes the instant-answer debugging payload.

suite.title: Test suite
suite.noRuns: No analysis runs yet for this dataset. Start one from the analysis page.
suite.captureRun: Reference capture — not scored against a reference
suite.columnsHint: Column names come from an optional testName column in the dataset. Tag rows with a caseType column (control, edge, boundary) to describe each test's role. Click a cell to open that answer.
